    AM Printservice

    Västervik, Sweden

    AM Printservice is a Swedish on-demand 3D printing service bureau headquartered in Västervik, producing parts in plastic and metal — single units to series of thousands — for automotive, drone, defence, aerospace and machinery customers across Sweden and Europe. Plastic in 5 working days, metal in 2–3 weeks.

    100kGarages

    Durham, United States

    100kGarages is a free online matchmaking marketplace that connects people with designs or ideas to independent digital fabricators ("Fabbers") who own CNC routers, laser cutters, and 3D printers. It was started in 2008 by ShopBot Tools, Inc. — one of the largest manufacturers of CNC digital fabrication tools in the US — in collaboration with Ponoko, and has since opened to owners of all brands of digital fabrication equipment. 100kGarages itself has no production capacity; orders are fulfilled by the individual Fabbers in its community. The site's 3D-printing offering is FDM-style printers running ABS and PLA filament; CNC routers and laser cutters cover wood, plastic, foam, aluminum and other soft metals, composites, acrylic, paper, and cardboard. The platform explicitly states that steel and other hard metals are NOT typical and recommends external machine shops for those needs.

    3D Explorer – MJF 3D Printing Service

    Rouse Hill, Australia

    3D Explorer is an Australian-owned MJF 3D printing service based in Rouse Hill, NSW, that operates HP Multi Jet Fusion production machines exclusively, printing every job in HP 3D High-Reusability CB PA 12. They also offer in-house AMT/PostPro Chemical Vapour Smoothing — a UL & CE certified vapour smoothing process that delivers injection-moulded surface quality and full sealing — applicable to their own MJF parts and to customer-supplied SLS, FDM, FFF and FGF parts. Applications span rapid prototyping, healthcare and orthopaedic models, architecture models, custom jigs and fixtures, consumer goods, prosthetics and orthotics, and arts. Members of the Advanced Manufacturing Growth Centre (AMGC) and the Australian Manufacturing Technology Institute Limited (AMTIL); sister brand venEyes offers fully customisable 3D-printed eyewear. Australia-wide shipping.

    3M

    3D Makers Zone

    Haarlem, Netherlands

    3D Makers Zone (3DMZ) is a Dutch innovation hub in Haarlem with one of the broadest additive manufacturing tech stacks in the Netherlands: large-format robotic FDM/FGF/LFAM, SLA/DLP resin, electron-beam metal AM (Freemelt ONE — first of its kind in NL, installed 2025), binder-jet stone/marble, and 3D concrete printing (Concr3de Armadillo). Officially opened 9 February 2016 at Oudeweg 91-95 in Haarlem and designated as a Smart Industry Fieldlab and European Digital Innovation Hub (EDIH) partner.

    3D MicroPrint

    Chemnitz, Germany

    Specialist in Micro Laser Sintering (µSLM) for micro-scale metal parts with precision down to 5-10 µm and wall thickness <50µm. Cooperation between EOS GmbH and 3D-Micromac AG, founded 2013. ISO 9001:2015 certified, ISO 13485 compliant. Focus: medical, aerospace, semiconductor.

    Delhi, India

    3D People UK

    London, United Kingdom

    3D People UK is an industrial additive manufacturing service provider headquartered in London, specializing in Multi Jet Fusion (MJF) and Selective Laser Sintering (SLS) technologies. Operating a state-of-the-art UK-based factory, they manufacture functional end-use components from polymer materials including PA12 Nylon and flexible TPU. The company serves diverse industries from aerospace and automotive to robotics and medical devices, offering comprehensive services from prototyping to high-volume production with ISO 9001 certified quality assurance.

    3D Print Bureau

    Stoke-on-Trent, United Kingdom

    3D Print Bureau is a UK industrial 3D printing service founded in 2015 and part of Tri-Tech Engineering Limited. Operating from a production HQ in Stoke-on-Trent with four satellite sites in Wales, Worcester, Bristol and Derby, they provide six additive manufacturing processes — FDM, PolyJet, SLA, DLP, polymer PBF (MJF/SAF) and metal Powder Bed Fusion — primarily on Stratasys equipment. Materials span engineering thermoplastics, photopolymer resins and metal powders for automotive, aerospace, medical, industrial, consumer-goods and product-design applications.

    3D Print Danmark – Custom 3D Printing & Rapid Prototyping

    Haderslev, Denmark

    3D Print Danmark is a Danish industrial 3D printing service operating two locations: headquarters at H.C. Ørsteds Vej 4, 6100 Haderslev and a satellite office at Havnegade 29, 2nd floor, 5000 Odense C. They run custom-built industrial FDM printers and SLA resin printers, producing 3,000–5,000 parts per month at 0.1 mm tolerance. Their FDM material range covers PLA, ABS, ASA, PETG, TPU, polypropylene (PP), nylon, plus carbon-fiber-reinforced filament, PVA support, HIPS, wood-filled, PBT+, PBA and bioplast; SLA printing uses standard photopolymer resin. Beyond printing they offer rapid prototyping, 3D CAD design (Autodesk Inventor 2018–2020, Meshmixer), CE-marking and full compliance documentation (technical drawings, risk assessment, user manuals, production files). Industries served include machine and motor parts, maritime equipment, process-optimization tooling, safety equipment, robotics, signage and architectural models. Contact: info@3dprintdanmark.dk, +45 53 56 44 00.

    3D Printing Ally – Rapid Prototyping & Production Services

    Cottage Grove, United States

    3D Printing Ally is a Cottage Grove, Minnesota rapid-prototyping and low-volume manufacturing service offering in-house FDM (Stratasys Fortus 400mc), SLA (3D Systems ProJet 6000 HD and 7000 SD), and SLS. The team emphasizes same-day quotes, same-day machine starts, and sales-engineering support to help customers select the right process and material. FDM materials include ABS-M30, Polycarbonate, and ULTEM 9085; SLA materials include Accura ClearVue, Accura Xtreme White 200, and Accura 25; SLS uses DuraForm PA (Nylon 12). Acquired by Intek Plastics in August 2025, 3D Printing Ally continues to operate under its own brand as part of Intek Plastics Group.

    3D Printing Leeds

    Leeds, United Kingdom

    3D Printing Leeds is a UK-based 3D print farm offering rapid prototyping, batch production and additive manufacturing for businesses, with over 30 years of in-house industrial design and engineering experience. They specialise in FDM (PLA, PETG, ABS, ASA, TPU and nylon) and LCD resin 3D printing, and also provide 3D scanning, CAD modelling and reverse engineering. Build envelopes are 300x300x300 mm for FDM and 80x40x100 mm for LCD resin. They serve automotive, product development, gaming, animation, medical simulation and assistive device clients across the UK.

With a wide range of technologies and over 90 materials, they serve engineers and businesses globally, helping to transform digital models into high-quality physical parts.

    3D Printing Singapore

    Singapore, Singapore

    3D Print Singapore is an industrial-grade 3D printing service provider established in 2020, offering five core technologies (FDM, SLA, SLS, MJF, and metal SLM) for rapid prototyping and production-grade manufacturing. The company serves Singapore's leading brands, government agencies, defence, transport, academic institutions, and research centres with fast turnaround times (as quick as 1 day) and comprehensive material options. Beyond 3D printing, they provide end-to-end product development services including CAD design, PCB engineering, industrial design, and embedded software consulting.

    3D Printing Tech

    Atlanta, United States

    3D Printing Tech is an Atlanta-based additive manufacturing service specializing in high-volume production and large-format 3D printing. Their print farm runs FDM/FFF for production-scale runs (8" to 14" cube build sizes plus a 12"x12"x24" bed), SLS in Nylon 12 (PA12), and Massivit Gel-Dispensing Printing (GDP) for life-size parts up to 70" x 57" x 43". Additional SLS plastics (PA11, PEEK, PP, TPU, TPE, PBT) and metal powder bed fusion in 316L, 17-4PH, M2 tool steel and Ti-6Al-4V are offered for large-volume contracts. Online instant quoting is available via a Digifabster integration, complemented by consultative project management for marketing models, jigs/fixtures, prototypes, film/TV props and short-run end-use parts.

    3S

    3D Systems

    Rock Hill, United States

    3D Systems (NYSE: DDD), founded in 1986 by 3D printing inventor Chuck Hull, is the original additive manufacturing company. Their portfolio spans Stereolithography (SLA), Selective Laser Sintering (SLS), MultiJet Printing (MJP), ColorJet Printing (CJP), Direct Metal Printing (DMP), Figure 4 projection-based DLP, PSLA projection stereolithography, and EXT Titan Pellet extrusion - plus dental (NextDent) and bioprinting platforms. Headquartered in Rock Hill, South Carolina, the company delivers printers, materials, software (3D Sprint, AddiTrak, Oqton) and application expertise to medical & dental, aerospace & defense, transportation & motorsports, AI infrastructure, and durable goods customers worldwide.

    Miami, United States

    3DChimera is a Miami, FL-based engineering-led 3D manufacturing studio that combines 3D printing (FDM/FFF, SLS, SLA), 3D scanning, CAD design and engineering, and turnkey 3D production systems. They specialise in solving real-world manufacturing problems - replacing broken parts, reverse-engineering discontinued components, short-run batch production, and digitising one-of-a-kind objects - for industries including aerospace, marine, automotive, architecture, art & design, and consumer goods.

    3DPRINTUK

    London, United Kingdom

    3DPRINTUK is a UK-based polymer 3D printing service headquartered at Unit D9, Leyton Industrial Village, Argall Ave, London E10 7QP. Founded in 2011 by CEO Nick Allen, the company operates the UK's largest powder-bed fusion (PBF) capacity for polymer parts: 12 × EOS Formiga P110/P100 SLS, 3 × EOS P396 SLS, 8 × HP 5210 Pro MJF, and 1 × Stratasys H350 SAF — 24 industrial printers in total. They offer four production materials (SLS Nylon PA12, MJF Nylon PA12, SAF PA11 ECO, SLS Flexible TPU) and five finishes (Natural, Vibro Polished, Shot Peened, Vapour Smoothed, Colour Dyed). 3DPRINTUK is ISO 9001:2015 and ISO 14001:2015 certified, JOSCAR accredited (aerospace, defence and security supply chain), and operates as a certified carbon-neutral company via ClimatePartner. Part of the TriMech Group. Express, Economy, Trade and Scheduled lead times are available, with express turnarounds in 2–4 working days. Contact: hello@3dprint-uk.co.uk, +44 (0)208 692 5208.

    3DPX

    Chicago, United States

    3DPX er en US-baseret kontraktproducent specialiseret i Selective Laser Sintering (SLS) og 3- og 5-akset CNC-bearbejdning. De producerer fra 1 til 10.000+ dele i PA2200 nylon og en bred vifte af metaller (aluminium, rustfrit stål, titanium, værktøjsstål, kobber) og tekniske polymerer (ABS, PC, PEEK, PP m.fl.) — med 1–5 dages standard leveringstid. Faciliteter i Chicago, IL og Columbia, SC; FDA-registreret medical device-producent og ISO 13485-certificeret.

    3DX Industries

    Ferndale, United States

    3DX Industries Inc. is a precision manufacturing company in Ferndale, WA that combines additive and subtractive processes under one roof. The additive division runs two ExOne M-Flex binder jet metal printers (with a Remet CFS 500 vacuum sintering furnace and bronze, copper or tin infiltration) plus FDM and MSLA polymer printers; the machine shop runs four 4-axis vertical machining centres up to 15,000 RPM and precision lathes. Materials include Stainless Steel 316/420, NanoSteel BLDRMetal J-10/J-11, Inconel 718 (HIP) for binder jet, and tool steel, aluminum, stainless steel, copper and titanium for CNC. Serves green/alternative energy, medical, aerospace, automotive and consumer/prototype industries. Public company (OTC: DDDX).

    3ERP – Rapid Prototyping & Low-Volume Manufacturing Services

    Zhongshan, China

    3ERP is an ISO 9001:2015 certified rapid prototyping and low-volume manufacturing company based in Zhongshan, Guangdong, China. They offer online instant-quote services for 3D printing (FDM, SLA, SLS, MJF, SLM, DMLS), CNC machining (3/4/5-axis milling, turning, EDM), injection molding including liquid silicone rubber, sheet metal fabrication, vacuum/urethane casting, rapid tooling, and pressure die casting. CNC tolerances down to ±0.01mm with 3-5 day delivery. Industries served include aerospace, automotive, medical, consumer products, robotics, and industrial machinery.

    3Faktur

    Jena, Germany

    3Faktur is an industrial 3D printing service provider headquartered at Göschwitzer Str. 22, 07745 Jena, Germany. They run three additive manufacturing technologies in-house: HP Multi Jet Fusion for polymer series production (PA 12, PA 12 W, PA 11 and BASF Ultrasint TPU01), metal laser melting (SLM) for stainless steel 316L and aluminum AlSi10Mg, and Precision Micro 3D Printing (PµSL) with HTL high-temperature resin for miniaturized and microfluidic parts. The company is ISO 9001 and ISO 52920 certified by TÜV Süd as an industrial additive manufacturing facility, produces over 150,000 additive series components per year for 2,500+ customers across mechanical engineering, medical technology, automotive, high-tech and orthopaedics, and offers express production, design-for-AM consulting, and a full finishing program (vapor smoothing, shot peening, dyeing, painting). Contact: info@3Faktur.com, +49 3641 225910-00.

    3rd Dimension Additive Manufacturing

    Augusta, United States

    3rd Dimension Additive Manufacturing is an FDM 3D printing service in Augusta, Georgia, founded in 2021 by Matt Anderson and Troy Flugaur. The company specializes in machine-ready, fiber-reinforced parts produced on industrial FDM printers, with material selection tuned for strength, flexibility, wear resistance, low friction, and electrical conductivity or insulation. They also offer mechanical CAD design support to take customers from concept and sketches through to a printable 3D model and prototype, with lead times measured in days and hours rather than weeks. Contact: 3daddmanufacturing@gmail.com, 706-842-8038 (call/text).

    3T Additive Manufacturing

    Thatcham, United Kingdom

    3T Additive Manufacturing er en UK-baseret leverandør specialiseret i avanceret metal additive manufacturing via Selective Laser Melting (SLM). De producerer færdige metalkomponenter og delsystemer i Inconel 718/625, Ti6Al4V, AlSi10Mg, Scalmalloy, 316L stainless og CoCr — fra design-optimering og udvikling gennem kvalificering til serieproduktion.     A3D Manufacturing, founded in 2019, is an on-demand digital manufacturing provider specializing in 3D printing, CNC machining, injection molding, and urethane casting. Based in Phoenix, Arizona, the company offers instant quoting and rapid production capabilities using HP Jet Fusion, FDM, SLS, SLA, and metal FFF technologies.     AddiThy

    Hurup Thy, Denmark

    AddiThy is a Danish additive manufacturing service founded in February 2024 and based in Hurup Thy. The two-founder team — Niklas Elowsson (AM specialist) and Tobias Ravnholt (mechanical engineer) — offers FFF/FDM (up to 36 × 36 × 36 cm), FGF large-format printing (up to 4.5 × 5 × 2.2 m), and SLS nylon (up to 16 × 16 × 30 cm). Services span 3D print production, product development consulting, custom solutions, training, and quality control. Materials include PLA, ABS, PETG, nylon (PA), PP, PPS and Ultem (PEI), plus UL94-certified plastics on request.

    additive tectonics GmbH

    Lupburg, Germany

    additive tectonics GmbH develops architectural 3D printing for the future of construction. Using particle bed 3D printing (SCA/SPI), the company produces large, complex concrete components without formwork — from structural elements to ornamental facades — at scales up to 10 m³. Services span computational design, structural engineering, execution planning, in-house additive manufacturing, and prefabrication for art, architecture, and design clients.

    Advantage Prototype Systems (APS3D)

    Sheboygan Falls, United States

    Advantage Prototype Systems (APS3D), founded in 1995 in Sheboygan Falls, Wisconsin, is a rapid prototyping service provider for engineers, product developers and artists. In-house capabilities cover Stereolithography (SLA, build envelope 25" x 29" x 21"), Fused Filament Fabrication (FFF/FDM) and Cast Urethane molding, with a strong focus on advanced finishing - vacuum metalizing (PVD), painting, clear/transparent finishes, dyeing, tinting and custom vinyl graphics. APS partners with outside providers to also supply SLS, MJF and DMLS parts. The company emphasises confidentiality of customer designs and on-time delivery on tight prototyping deadlines.

    United States

    American Additive Manufacturing provides advanced additive manufacturing services, specializing in rapid prototyping, large-scale production, and custom part manufacturing. They leverage cutting-edge FDM, SLA, SLS, MJF, DMLS, and SLM technologies to deliver precision-engineered solutions. The company is committed to supporting the 'Made in USA' initiative and improving supply chains for various industries.

    Visit

    AML3D

    North Plympton, Australia

    AML3D Limited (ASX:AL3) is an Australian metal additive manufacturing company headquartered in North Plympton, SA, with a US production hub in Stow, OH. They invented and patented the WAM® (Wire Additive Manufacturing) DED-arc process and sell it productised as ARCEMY® — described on aml3d.com as the world's largest open-air, production-ready commercial metal 3D printers (Small, X, XL, Enterprise and EDU editions, paired with AMLSoft™ and WAMSoft® software). Founded 2014 by Andy Sales; ASX listed April 2020. Materials span aluminium, titanium, copper alloys (incl. Nickel Aluminium Bronze for maritime), nickel superalloys (Inconel 625/718/622), Invar, steel and stainless/duplex grades — close to 30 feedstocks in total. Certified to AS9100D (aerospace), ISO 9001:2015, DNV (first WAM company globally approved for maritime steel & copper alloys), Lloyd's Register (world's first AM Facility Qualification, 2018), and AWS D20.1/D20.1M:2019 (Class A, B, C). Serves Aerospace, Defence, Maritime, Manufacturing, Oil & Gas, and Education + Research.

    DED
    WAAM
    Commercially Pure Titanium
    Invar 36
    Nickel Aluminum Bronze

    Amuse3D

    Chennai, India

    End-to-end manufacturing hub offering 3D printing, injection moulding, MJF printing, rapid prototyping, and CNC machining services.

    AH

    Amuse3D HP

    Chennai, India

    HP Multi Jet Fusion division of Amuse3D, focused on industrial-grade MJF 3D printing for serial production parts.

    AR

    Applied Rapid Technologies

    Fredericksburg, United States

    Applied Rapid Technologies (ART) is a Fredericksburg, Virginia-based rapid prototyping and short-run production service bureau founded in 1996 and operating since 2021 as a subsidiary of Obsidian Solutions Group. ART specializes in plastics-focused additive manufacturing, running a Nexa3D resin printer fleet alongside stereolithography, FDM, SLS, DMLS and PolyJet services for aerospace, defense, medical device, consumer goods, and sporting goods clients. Achieved CMMC Level 2 certification from the U.S. Department of Defense in March 2025.

    SLS
    SLA
    FDM

    Standard Resin
    Tough Resin
    Flexible Resin
    +4
    Visit
    A

    ARCreo

    Modena, Italy

    ARCreo is a metal additive-manufacturing brand based in Modena, Italy, operated by Guidetti Technology S.r.l. (Società Unipersonale) — a spin-off of the Arroweld Italia S.p.A. group dedicated to additive manufacturing. ARCreo's proprietary "ARCadditive" technology is a wire-arc additive process (WAAM) that melts a wire with an electric arc and deposits it layer-by-layer to build metal parts, using Fronius TPSi 400 CMT and iWAVE 400 Pro Additive welding generators. The product range includes the Weld3X (3-axis printer, Ø500 × 800 mm build volume) and the Weld6X (6-axis collaborative robot, 1300 or 1900 mm reach), plus a conversion service that turns an existing welder into a 3D printer. ARCreo also runs an in-house 3D-printing service from its demo room in Modena, producing high-quality samples, small / limited-edition batches, and on-demand parts in metal alloys, with a free 30-minute consultation available on request. Quotes are form-based (no live quoting tool). Headquartered at Via Gaetano Salvemini, 16/18, 41123 Modena (MO), Italy. Phone +39 059 314353, email info@arcreo.it.

    WAAM
    Visit
    AE

    Arinta Engineering

    Sturtevant, United States

    Arinta Engineering is a Racine, Wisconsin-based company specializing in new product development, engineering design, and metal fabrication. Their facility is equipped with advanced 3D printers, precision plasma cutters, and expert welding capabilities, enabling them to produce high-quality metal products. They cater to individuals, inventors, startups, and small businesses, focusing on low-volume, custom metal parts.

    FDM
    Visit
    A

    Articon

    Irvine, United States

    Articon specializes in providing high-precision dental digital solutions, including milled titanium implant bars and a range of dental 3D printers, resins, and milling machines. Based in California, the company focuses on delivering products with exceptional fit, strength, and fast turnaround times for dental labs and clinicians. They offer a comprehensive suite of dental manufacturing technologies and materials.

    AB

    Aspect Biosystems

    Vancouver, Canada

    Aspect Biosystems is a Canadian biotechnology company pioneering the development of bioprinted tissue therapeutics. They utilize a full-stack platform integrating AI-powered bioprinting technology, computational design tools, therapeutic cells, and advanced biomaterials. Their mission is to create implantable tissues that replace, repair, or supplement biological functions, aiming to transform the treatment of various diseases.

    Visit
    AM

    Autotiv Manufacturing

    Salem, United States

    Autotiv Manufacturing is a digital manufacturing company headquartered at 142 Main Street, Building B, Salem, NH 03079, USA. They operate 200+ machines across five in-house and global services: 3D printing (SLS, SLA, FDM, DMLS, PolyJet, MJF — 100+ materials, parts from $0.07), CNC machining (plastics, metals, composites, ±0.001" tolerances, parts from $0.70), injection molding (plastics, elastomers, LSR with insert/overmolding, parts from $0.01), sheet-metal fabrication (4–26 gauge cutting, bending, welding, parts from $0.30), and urethane casting (rigid or flexible polyurethanes, ~20 parts/mold, parts from $0.90). They offer instant interactive quotes via make.autotiv.com, 1-day expedited lead times with a 4:30 PM EST cutoff, free DFM and simulation on injection molding, and back every order with a Quality Guarantee. The company combines domestic capacity in New Hampshire with a tariff-optimised global supply chain, and counts SharkNinja, MIT, Formlabs and Brigham & Women's among its customers. Contact: contact@autotiv.com, (888) 369-5442.

    B

    B9Creations

    Rapid City, United States

    B9Creations is a U.S.-based digital manufacturing company headquartered in Rapid City, South Dakota, that designs and manufactures DLP-based 3D printers, photopolymer resins, and software, plus a Parts-on-Demand contract manufacturing service. The B9 Core, B9 Core Med, B9 Dent, B9 Elite (micro) and B9 6 Series platforms — together with FAST(TM) printing technology — serve high-precision applications in aerospace and defense, medical and biomedical engineering, dental, jewelry, prototyping and manufacturing, microscale, education and research, and entertainment. Materials range from engineering-grade (Rugged Nylon-6, Robust ABS, ESD Rigid, Resilient Silicone) and casting (Yellow, Emerald, FastWax, Cherry, Red) to biocompatible ISO 10993 BioRes families (BioRes-Silicone, BioRes-Clear, BioRes-Micro Precision, BioWhite, BioRed), dental (Model, Keystone Gingival Mask, Keystone Surgical Guide, DENTCA Crown & Bridge), and specialty resins (ATARU Black for high-temperature RF, Radix Printable Dielectric, Loctite 3D 3843, Loctite 3D IND147, bioinks). Customers include Johnson & Johnson, Hasbro, Stuller, Medtronic and Procter & Gamble, with certified dealers in nearly 70 countries.

    BaessTech Engineering (BaessTech GmbH) is a German 3D printing service provider based in Wackersdorf, Bavaria. Founded in 2020, they offer FDM, SLA, SLS, and MJF printing alongside full product development services — from CAD design and topology optimization through manufacturing, branding, and marketplace launch.

    Baesstech-Engineering

    Germany

    Baesstech-Engineering is a German company specializing in innovative product development and additive manufacturing. They guide clients from initial product ideas through design, 3D printing, and even marketing strategies. The company offers a comprehensive service, including construction, 3D printing of prototypes and small series, and start-up support for successful market launch.

    Visit

    Baker Industries

    Macomb, United States

    Baker Industries, a Lincoln Electric Company, is an award-winning contract manufacturer headquartered in Macomb, Michigan, specializing in large-scale, low-volume production. Founded in 1992 and now operating across five plants totaling 250,000+ ft², they combine 3-, 4-, 5-, and 7-axis CNC machining, fabrication and welding, and proprietary Wire Arc Additive Manufacturing (WAAM) — printing metal parts up to 8 feet tall and over 5,000 lbs using SculptPrint OS path-planning software. AS9100D, ISO 9001:2015 and ISO 14001:2015 certified, ITAR registered and Nadcap (AC7130) accredited. Serves aerospace, defense, space, automotive, energy and heavy-equipment customers including Boeing, Lockheed Martin, Rocket Lab, GKN Aerospace, GM and Stellantis.

    BEAMIT

    Not explicitly stated, Italy

    BEAMIT is a leading global additive manufacturing service provider specializing in high-end metal 3D printing components for demanding aerospace, space, defense, energy, automotive, and biomedical industries. With 25+ years of AM expertise, 120+ employees, 7 dedicated plants, 59 machines, and partnerships with Sandvik and Elementum 3D, BEAMIT offers a fully integrated value chain from design and material development through post-processing and quality control. The company recently joined Rainshow Industries Group to further strengthen its position as a one-stop-shop for complex AM components with multiple certifications and accreditations.

    EBM
    SLM
    Visit
    BP

    BigRep Partlab

    Berlin, Germany

    BigRep Partlab is the on-demand 3D printing service from BigRep GmbH, producing large-format FFF parts up to one cubic metre on BigRep's own industrial printers. Customers upload STL/STEP/OBJ files, receive a quote within 48 hours, and the team prints, post-processes and ships the parts. Materials span standard polymers (PLA, PETG, ABS, ASA), engineering-grade nylons (PA6/66) and TPU 98A, plus fibre-reinforced composites (PA12 CF, HI-TEMP CF).

    Bone 3D

    Paris, France

    Bone 3D is a French medtech founded in 2018, specializing in 3D printing for healthcare. They produce anatomical models, surgical simulators, and medical devices using a Stratasys J750 (multi-material PolyJet) and Formlabs Form 2/Form 3 (SLA). They deployed 60 Stratasys printers across Europe's largest hospital system (AP-HP Paris) and serve 100+ healthcare customers.

    Boston Micro Fabrication (BMF)

    Boston, United States

    Boston Micro Fabrication (BMF) is a global leader in advanced additive manufacturing, specializing in Projection Micro Stereolithography (PµSL) technology. The company enables the 3D printing of true microstructures with ultra-high resolution (2µm~50µm) and precision. BMF's solutions provide complete design freedom for intricate, exact, and replicable parts at a scale and tolerance comparable to micro injection molded parts.

    Burloak Technologies

    Oakville, Canada

    Burloak Technologies, a Samuel, Son & Co. company headquartered in Oakville, Ontario, is one of the largest metal additive manufacturing contract manufacturers in North America. Operating an Additive Manufacturing Center of Excellence with a fleet of EOS, Renishaw, Trumpf, SLM Solutions, GE Arcam, Optomec and Essentium platforms, Burloak combines L-PBF/SLM, EBM, DED and SLS printing with NADCAP-accredited heat treatment (including Canada's most advanced HIP vessel), multi-axis CNC machining and surface treatment, and an ISO 17025-accredited materials and metrology lab. AS9100D, ISO 9001, NADCAP and ISO 17025 certified, and registered under the Canadian Controlled Goods Program (CGP). Serves space, aviation, military & defense and energy customers with extensive metal AM flight heritage.

    CA Models

    Stirling, United Kingdom

    CA Models is a prototyping pioneer established in 1983, offering market-leading precision engineering and additive manufacturing services across the UK and internationally. The company operates a uniquely broad range of 3D printing technologies including SLA, SLS, FDM, multi-material printing, and metal additive manufacturing (DMLS), complemented by CNC machining, vacuum casting, and comprehensive finishing services. With three of the largest capacity DMLS systems in the world and a skilled team of nine model makers, CA Models combines advanced in-house technologies with traditional craftsmanship to deliver high-precision prototypes and production parts.

The company's advanced ceramic manufacturing addresses challenges in industries such as fluid handling, chemical processing, energy, and industrial machinery, including 3D printed ceramic solutions.

    Carbon

    Redwood City, United States

    Carbon is a leading additive manufacturing company pioneering Digital Light Synthesis (DLS) technology for high-performance 3D printing. Founded in 2013, Carbon designs and manufactures advanced photopolymer resins, computational design software, and 3D printing hardware enabling brands to create radically better products. The company serves diverse industries including consumer goods, sporting goods, dental, medical devices, and automotive with a focus on precision, speed, and material performance.

    COBOD

    Taastrup, Denmark

    COBOD International is a Danish-based pioneer in 3D construction printing, developing and manufacturing robotic 3D construction printers for the global market. Founded with a mission to address the global housing crisis and labor shortage in construction, COBOD has grown exponentially since 2018, tripling revenues to 83.4M DKK in 2021 and selling 30+ printers globally. The company partners with industry leaders including GE Renewable Energy, CEMEX, Holcim, and PERI Group to advance automation, robotics, and sustainable construction technologies.

    Craftcloud – 3D Printing & Manufacturing Platform

    Munich, Germany

    Craftcloud is a 3D printing and manufacturing service marketplace operated by All3DP GmbH (Munich, Germany). Founded in 2016, it connects customers with a curated network of 150+ manufacturing partners across 15 countries and delivers to 95+ countries, providing instant cross-vendor price comparison for FDM, SLA, SLS, MJF, metal AM, CNC machining, sheet metal fabrication, injection molding and casting services. Used by 140,000+ businesses and 75+ universities and colleges.

The institute focuses on delivering innovative solutions and knowledge to Danish companies, particularly in areas like food contact applications and quality assurance.

Design Roots serves diverse industries including aerospace, automotive, medical, electronics, and consumer goods across India, delivering functional, production-ready parts with accessible pricing and expert technical support.

    Fathom Manufacturing – Advanced 3D Printing & On-Demand Manu

    Multiple locations nationwide, United States

    Fathom Manufacturing is a comprehensive advanced manufacturing provider offering 25+ technologies including 3D printing, CNC machining, injection molding, and sheet metal fabrication. Founded in 2008 and expanded through strategic acquisitions, Fathom represents over 250 years of combined manufacturing expertise. The company specializes in rapid prototyping, low-volume/high-mix production, and complex assemblies for aerospace, medical, automotive, and other regulated industries, with white-glove engineering support from concept to production.

    CNC Machining

    Visit

    Fictiv

    Oakland, United States

    Fictiv is a global digital manufacturing platform headquartered in Oakland, California, founded in 2013 by brothers Dave Evans (CEO) and Nathan Evans (Chief Experience Officer). They deliver custom CNC machining (3-, 4- and 5-axis milling, turning, EDM, gear hobbing), 3D printing (FDM, SLS, SLA, PolyJet, MJF, Carbon DLS), injection molding, sheet metal, die casting, compression molding, and urethane casting through in-house manufacturing centers in the U.S., Mexico (Santa Catarina), India (Bengaluru, Pune), and China (Guangzhou). Instant online quoting, automated DFM feedback, and order management run through a single platform. CNC parts ship as fast as 1 day (aluminum), 3D-printed parts as fast as 24 hours, and injection molding T1 samples in as fast as 10 days. Fictiv itself is ISO 9001:2015 certified; its partner network covers AS9100 Rev D (aerospace), ISO 13485:2016 (medical), and IATF 16949:2016 (automotive). Fictiv does not support ITAR-classified work.

    Go 3D Print

    Manchester, United Kingdom

    Go 3D Print is a Manchester-based 3D printing specialist dedicated to transforming digital designs into high-quality physical products. Founded on the belief that innovative ideas deserve precision manufacturing, the company offers comprehensive additive manufacturing services including rapid prototyping, custom product design, and small-batch production. With a team of design engineers and product designers, they bridge the gap between concept and tangible reality with fast turnaround times and exceptional quality standards.

    Rapid Prototyping

    GoEngineer – Industrial 3D Printing & Additive Manufacturing

    Midvale, United States

    GoEngineer is a leading provider of 3D printing services and additive manufacturing solutions with over 40 years of experience in product design and engineering. Operating 100+ 3D printers across the United States, the company specializes in FDM, PolyJet, Stereolithography, SAF, and P3 technologies. GoEngineer serves aerospace, defense, medical, and industrial sectors with ITAR-registered, ISO 9001:2015 and AS9100 certified manufacturing capabilities, offering instant online quoting and rapid prototyping to production-grade parts.

    igus – Wear-Resistant 3D Printing Service

    Providence, United States

    igus® is a leading provider of high-performance polymer solutions and 3D printing services specializing in wear-resistant additive manufacturing. With over 30 years of expertise in tribology and self-lubricating materials, igus® offers 3D printed parts that last up to 50 times longer than standard 3D printing materials. The company operates a 41,000 square foot test laboratory and provides fast service with parts delivered in 2-3 business days, no minimum order quantity required.

The company specializes in cutting-edge precision component manufacturing combining conventional CNC 5-axis machining, non-conventional machining (ECM, EDM, laser, waterjet), and advanced additive manufacturing technologies including directed energy deposition, direct metal deposition, electron beam melting, and laser powder bed fusion.     Materialise Manufacturing

    Leuven, Belgium

    Materialise Manufacturing is a professional online 3D printing service operated by Materialise NV from its Leuven, Belgium headquarters. With 140+ in-house 3D printers and 45+ materials across eight industrial technologies — SLS, SLA, MJF, FDM, Metal 3D printing (SLM), PolyJet, vacuum (urethane) casting and lost-wax casting — they offer instant quoting via the OnSite portal and a Fast Lane 48-hour service for selected materials. Production is ISO 9001-certified and SOX-compliant. The legacy i.materialise consumer brand has been absorbed into this unified Materialise Manufacturing service.

    NT

    Norsk Titanium

    Oslo, Norway

    Norsk Titanium is a pioneering additive manufacturing company specializing in Rapid Plasma Deposition (RPD®), a proprietary wire-arc technology for structural titanium parts. The company has achieved FAA approval and OEM qualification for aerospace applications, delivering parts with 50-100x faster production speeds than powder-based systems while using 25-50% less material than traditional forging. With facilities in Oslo, Norway and Plattsburgh, New York, Norsk Titanium serves the aerospace and defense sectors with high-volume, on-demand metal manufacturing capabilities.

    Titanium
    Visit
    N

    NUGAE

    NUGAE specializes in large-format robotic 3D printing, leveraging over 10 years of R&D to produce ultra-light, strong, and recyclable composite thermoplastic components. Their technology enables the creation of structural elements up to 3m x 1.5m x 1m, offering significant weight reduction and enhanced resistance to marine agents. NUGAE focuses on delivering innovative solutions for various applications, including core elements for sandwich structures, ready-to-use prototypes, and molds.

    Oceanz is an ISO9001 & ISO13485 certified 3D printing company based in the Netherlands, specializing in rapid production of high-quality plastic and metal parts. They offer a broad range of 3D printing techniques and post-processing options, serving industries such as medical, industrial, food & agri, and aerospace.

    Oerlikon AM

    Pfäffikon, Switzerland

    Oerlikon is a global leader in surface technologies and advanced materials with a dedicated Additive Manufacturing division (Oerlikon AM). The company engineers innovative technologies and solutions for applications in aerospace, automotive, energy, medical, and semiconductor industries. Oerlikon AM provides customized additive manufacturing solutions including metal 3D printing, advanced powders, and related services for industrial applications. With approximately 9,300 employees across 38 countries and 2025 sales of CHF 1.6 billion, Oerlikon combines deep material science expertise with manufacturing capabilities.

    Visit
    OF

    OK Foundry Company, Inc.

    Richmond, United States

    OK Foundry Company, Inc. is a family-owned and operated jobbing foundry established in 1912 in Richmond, Virginia. Specializing in gray and ductile iron sand castings, the company produces parts ranging from 1 lb. to 400 lbs. using phenolic urethane bonded sand molding. They serve general industrial needs for both short runs and serial production, continuously evolving with modern casting technologies.

    Visit
    OO

    One Off Robotics

    Chattanooga, United States

    One Off Robotics is a direct energy deposition (DED) and wire arc additive manufacturing (WAAM) specialist providing production-ready metal additive solutions. The company manufactures large-format metal components using proprietary closed-loop thermal machine vision technology that continuously monitors and adjusts printing parameters in real-time. One Off Robotics serves aerospace, defense, marine, energy, and mining industries with both on-demand WAAM printing services and turnkey robotic additive manufacturing systems including the Monarc production platform and field-deployable Nomad system.

    WAAM

The lab provides access to world-class equipment for students, faculty, and the public.

The company operates as a turnkey manufacturing partner offering additive manufacturing services across 12+ technologies and 90+ materials, serving over 1 million customers across 180+ countries with 24+ million parts manufactured. Shapeways enables hardware innovators, startups, and enterprises to prototype, launch, and scale products from single units to 10,000+ parts without minimum order quantities, combining proprietary 3D printing expertise with design optimization, production support, and lifecycle management.

    Sintavia

    Hollywood, United States

    Sintavia is the world's first all-digital aerospace component manufacturer specializing in additively designed and manufactured Power & Thermal Management Systems (PTMS) for aerospace and defense applications. The company operates 23 large and extra-large industrial metal printers co-located with precision post-processing equipment, mechanical testing facilities, and a fully accredited metallurgical and powder laboratory. Sintavia's vertical integration of design, fabrication, quality testing, and post-processing enables production of complex thermodynamic systems including heat exchangers, turbomachinery components, advanced fuel systems, and combustion chambers that are impossible to replicate using traditional manufacturing methods.

    Storme Technologies

    Jefferson, United States

    Wisconsin-based 3D printing and prototyping company with 40+ printers, offering industrial solutions from prototyping to large-scale manufacturing.

    Stratasys Direct

    Eden Prairie, United States

    Stratasys Direct is the on-demand manufacturing service of Stratasys, headquartered at 7665 Commerce Way, Eden Prairie, MN. With over 30 years of additive manufacturing experience and three U.S. Centers of Excellence in Eden Prairie (MN), Belton (TX), and Tucson (AZ), they offer eight industrial 3D printing technologies — FDM, PolyJet, P3 DLP, SLA, SAF, SLS, HP MJF, and VCJ — across 50+ engineering-grade materials. All facilities are ISO 9001, AS9100, and ISO 13485 certified, with ITAR/DDTC registration for defense work. Standard lead time is 3 days; capacity includes 200+ in-house printers and over 24 million parts produced.

    Studiobenkert specializes in large-scale 3D printing, offering innovative and precise solutions. They focus on using recycled and upcycled materials, emphasizing sustainability and German manufacturing quality. The company aims to make creative visions tangible through their advanced 3D printing capabilities.

    SyBridge On-Demand (formerly Fast Radius)

    Rancho Cucamonga, United States

    SyBridge Technologies is a global precision tooling and specialty manufacturing company with over 50 years of mold-making expertise. As the #2 moldmaker in North America with 17 locations across 5 countries, SyBridge operates one of North America's fastest-growing 3D printing production facilities delivering high-quality, end-use parts at scale. The company provides comprehensive solutions spanning injection mold tooling design, pre-production and production tooling, validation services, quick-turn molded parts, and advanced 3D printing manufacturing.

    Synergeering Group

    Farmington Hills, United States

    Synergeering Group is a direct service bureau specializing in large and durable additive manufacturing and 3D rapid prototype parts. They are known for their proprietary RapidNylon® material, which is suitable for high-heat, airtight, and chemical-resistant applications. The company focuses on providing fully functional prototype parts.

    Tesseract is a comprehensive product development partner based in Mumbai, India, offering 3D printing, prototyping, and full-scale manufacturing services. With 87+ machines and 437+ customers across 17+ industries, the company specializes in transforming ideas into reality through cutting-edge design, advanced prototyping, and top-tier manufacturing capabilities. Their expert team delivers solutions ranging from rapid prototyping to production-scale manufacturing with quick turnaround times and instant quoting.

    Unterfahlheim, Germany

    TEUFEL Prototypen GmbH specializes in additive manufacturing services including Stereolithography (SLA), Lasersintering (SLS), and DLP. They also offer vacuum casting, silicone casting, rapid tooling, and high-speed cutting for prototypes and small batch series. The company focuses on producing high-quality plastic parts and metal samples.

    toolcraft AG

    Georgensgmünd, Germany

    toolcraft AG is a German precision manufacturing company established in 1989 with 482 employees specializing in additive manufacturing, CNC machining, injection molding, and robotics automation. Operating from facilities in Georgensgmünd and Spalt, Bavaria, the company offers integrated manufacturing solutions combining metal 3D printing (DMLS, SLM), subtractive machining, and custom automation systems. toolcraft serves aerospace, medical, automotive, and defense industries with high-end precision components manufactured under strict quality certifications including AS9100, ISO 13485, and NADCAP.

    Toray (Metal 3D Printing Service)

    Japan

    Toray Precision is a pioneer in ultra-precision microfabrication technology, with a history dating back to 1955. The company specializes in manufacturing key components for synthetic fiber production and has expanded its expertise to various industrial fields. They offer advanced microfabrication services, including metal 3D printing, and provide solutions for complex manufacturing challenges.

    SLM
    Visit

    Treatstock

    Newark, United States

    Treatstock is an online manufacturing marketplace and e-commerce platform headquartered in Newark, Delaware. Founded in 2016, the platform connects buyers with a global network of vetted manufacturers offering 3D printing (FDM, SLA, SLS, MJF, SLM, DLP, Material Jetting, DMLS), CNC machining (milling, turning), cutting (laser, CO₂, plasma, waterjet), injection molding, sheet metal, urethane casting, metal casting (die / investment), vacuum forming, mold making, metal stamping, 3D scanning, and 3D design services across more than 25 plastic, metal, composite, and organic materials. Customers upload a CAD file, get an instant online quote, compare suppliers worldwide, and order with buyer protection. Treatstock also operates an e-commerce store for ready-to-print 3D models and provides API integrations, manufacturing tools, and an advertising network for partner suppliers. Trusted by Apple, Tesla, BMW, Stanford, Google, Samsung, Adobe, Bayer, Verizon, Huawei, Deloitte, and others.

Founded by the leadership of 3Diligent, Trustbridge offers DFM support, supplier matchmaking, and project management - they are not a manufacturer themselves but coordinate production across 300+ additive partners worldwide.

    Ultimate 3D Printing Services – Professional 3D Printing & A

    Odessa, United States

    Ultimate 3D Printing Services is a USA-based, family-owned additive manufacturing facility founded in 2015, headquartered in Pasco County, Florida. The company specializes in rapid prototyping and production-scale 3D printing, offering access to over 300 materials and 7 distinct printing technologies. They serve diverse industries from aerospace to medical devices, combining advanced manufacturing capabilities with personalized design and consulting services to bring manufacturing back to America.

    Unionfab

    China

    Unionfab is China's largest 3D printing and CNC manufacturing company, specializing in rapid prototyping and on-demand production parts. They offer a wide range of additive manufacturing technologies including SLA, FDM, SLS, MJF, and metal 3D printing, alongside extensive CNC machining capabilities. With over 800 industrial-grade machines, Unionfab serves various industries with diverse material options and finishing services.

    U

    Uptive

    Multiple locations, United States

    UPTIVE is a disruptive rapid manufacturing company formed through the merger of RE3DTECH and GoProto in 2021-2022, with additional acquisitions including Stanfordville Machine and Manufacturing (founded 1975) and Phoenix Tool & Mold (founded 2008). The company operates 5 locations across the US and Mexico, offering comprehensive digital manufacturing solutions combining advanced additive manufacturing (3D printing) with traditional manufacturing processes including CNC machining, sheet metal fabrication, injection molding, and urethane casting. UPTIVE differentiates itself through personalized, customer-centric service with expert engineering guidance, design-for-manufacturability consultation, and long-term partnership focus rather than automated quoting.

    V

    Vertico

    Eindhoven, Netherlands

    Vertico is a specialized 3D concrete printing solutions provider headquartered in Eindhoven, Netherlands. The company designs and manufactures fully integrated concrete 3D printing systems featuring proprietary printheads, 6-axis robotic platforms, and advanced slicing software. Vertico serves design architects, construction firms, and educational institutions globally, with 51 printers sold across 24 countries and 6 continents. Their mission is to create a new paradigm in design, architecture, and construction by removing barriers of conventional manufacturing.

    3D Concrete Printing
    Concrete
    Visit

    Vision Miner

    Beaverton, United States

    Vision Miner is a leading additive manufacturing supplier specializing in high-performance 3D printing systems, advanced materials, and professional services. The company offers a comprehensive portfolio including FDM printers (22 IDEX, AON 3D Hylo), SLA systems (Formlabs Form 4/4L), and SLS technology (Fuse 1+), alongside premium engineering polymers like PEEK, ULTEM, and carbon-fiber composites. Vision Miner provides complete solutions for prototyping and end-use part production, backed by expert consultation, training, and custom 3D printing services for industries requiring extreme heat resistance, chemical durability, and high-strength materials.

    Visit

    voxeljet

    Augsburg, Germany

    voxeljet is a global innovator and technology driver for advanced 3D printing systems and solutions in production environments. The company specializes in binder-jetting and High Speed Sintering (HSS) technologies for industrial-scale additive manufacturing. Equipped with state-of-the-art components seamlessly integrated into partially or fully automated production environments, voxeljet's 3D production systems shift the economic parameters of additive manufacturing. The company manufactures the world's largest 3D printing systems, including the VX4000 with a 4x2x1 meter build volume, and offers both equipment sales and on-demand 3D printing services globally.

    VulcanForms

    Devens and Newburyport, United States

    VulcanForms is an advanced metal manufacturing platform that combines laser powder bed fusion (LPBF) additive manufacturing with precision CNC machining to produce complex, high-performance metal parts. Founded to serve aerospace, defense, medical, and industrial sectors, the company operates digital factories with integrated design, simulation, and quality control capabilities. VulcanForms specializes in flight-critical aerospace components, medical implants, and high-strength industrial parts using titanium, inconel, stainless steel, and other advanced alloys. The company maintains U.S.-based facilities in Massachusetts with ITAR certification and AS9100D aerospace compliance.

    Milton Keynes, United Kingdom

    WAAM3D specializes in Wire Arc Additive Manufacturing (WAAM) technologies, offering integrated systems like RoboWAAM and MiniWAAM for large-scale industrial metal components. They provide end-to-end software solutions for tool path planning, simulation, and process control. The company serves industries such as aerospace, energy, marine, and R&D, focusing on cost reduction, waste minimization, and improved material properties.

    Weerg – 3D Printing & CNC Machining On-Demand Manufacturing

    Venice, Italy

    Weerg is an Italian on-demand manufacturing provider specializing in 3D printing, CNC machining, and laser cutting services. Operating from a 10,000 m² factory in Venice, the company operates the world's largest fleet of 28 HP Multi Jet Fusion 5620 Pro printers, enabling production of 1 to 100,000 functional parts in as little as 2-3 days. All production is handled in-house with end-to-end file encryption, offering instant online quoting and rapid turnaround times with 50+ material options.

    WhiteClouds

    Utah, United States

    WhiteClouds is a next-generation 3D fabrication company specializing in custom on-demand models, displays, and consumer products for businesses and consumers. Founded with innovation, craftsmanship, and creativity as core values, the company operates one of the largest 3D printing facilities globally with 60,000 square feet of production space in Utah. WhiteClouds combines six different 3D printing technologies with traditional fabrication methods including foam cutting, CNC machining, welding, and sculpting to create everything from the world's largest 3D-printed 30-foot statue to intricate topographical models and architectural displays.

Xometry serves diverse industries such as aerospace, automotive, medical, and consumer products, providing solutions from rapid prototyping to high-volume production.

They provide 3D printing in FDM, SLS, SLA, MJM, MJF, PolyJet, DMLS, and SLM, plus CNC machining, vacuum casting, injection moulding, laser cutting, and 3D scanning.     ZELTA3D

    Singapore, Singapore

    Singapore-based industrial 3D printing service with ISO 9001:2015 certification, using EOS SLS and HP MJF 5210 with 1,000,000+ parts produced.

    Visit

    ZiggZagg

    Aalter, Belgium

    Ziggzagg is a Belgian B2B 3D printing service provider headquartered at Venecolaan 10, 9880 Aalter, with over 10 years of experience in on-demand additive manufacturing. They operate one of the largest 3D printing fleets in Belgium, offering HP Multi Jet Fusion (MJF), Selective Laser Sintering (SLS), Selective Laser Melting (SLM), Stereolithography (SLA), and proprietary Cold Metal Fusion (CMF) for metal parts. Materials include PA12, PA11, PA12 GF, TPU, SLA resins (standard and clear), 316L stainless steel, 17-4PH stainless steel, AlSi10Mg aluminum, and titanium. Customers order via the online platform (order.ziggzagg.be) with instant quotes, 1-3 working day express service for PA12/PA11/TPU, next-day delivery within 300 km of Belgium, and worldwide shipping. ISO 9001 and ISO 13485 certified, winner of the 2024 Agoria & Sirris 'Factory of the Future' award. Minimum order value €100. Customers include Audi, Tesla, Volvo, Honda, ASML, BASF, Atlas Copco, Medtronic, Johnson & Johnson, Samsonite, and the Belgian Defense. Contact: info@ziggzagg.be, +32 9 325 90 00, VAT BE 0826.187.503.
